title: Modern Animated Sign-In Components
content:
This file provides a set of reusable React components designed to create modern and animated user interfaces, particularly for sign-in and form-related functionalities. ## Key Components ### `Input` A visually enhanced input field with a radial gradient hover effect. It uses `motion` from `framer-motion` to create a dynamic visual response to mouse movement. **Props:** - Accepts all standard `React.InputHTMLAttributes`. - `className`: Additional CSS classes for styling. - `type`: Input type (e.g., 'text', 'password'). ### `Label` A simple, styled label component for form elements. **Props:** - Accepts all standard `React.LabelHTMLAttributes`. - `htmlFor`: Specifies the input element the label is associated with. - `className`: Additional CSS classes for styling. ### `BoxReveal` A component that animates the appearance of its children with a sliding box effect. It uses `useAnimation` and `useInView` hooks to trigger animations when the element comes into view. **Props:** - `children`: The content to be revealed. - `width`: The width of the container. - `boxColor`: The color of the reveal box. - `duration`: The animation duration. - `overflow`: CSS overflow property. - `position`: CSS position property. - `className`: Additional CSS classes. ### `Ripple` Creates a pulsating ripple effect using multiple animated `span` elements. This component is often used for visual feedback or decorative elements. **Props:** - `mainCircleSize`: The base size of the largest ripple circle. - `mainCircleOpacity`: The opacity of the largest ripple circle. - `numCircles`: The total number of ripple circles to render. - `className`: Additional CSS classes. ### `OrbitingCircles` A component that arranges children in orbiting circles. It can optionally display a guiding path (SVG circle) and supports reverse animation direction. **Props:** - `children`: The elements to be placed in orbit. - `reverse`: If true, reverses the animation direction. - `duration`: The duration of the orbit animation. - `delay`: The delay before the animation starts. - `radius`: The radius of the orbit. - `path`: If true, renders an SVG circle as a path indicator. - `className`: Additional CSS classes. ### `TechOrbitDisplay` Combines `OrbitingCircles` to display a set of technology icons or components in an orbiting fashion around a central text element. **Props:** - `iconsArray`: An array of `IconConfig` objects, each defining an icon's animation properties and the component to render. - `text`: The central text to display. **`IconConfig` Interface:** - `className`: Additional CSS classes for the orbiting icon. - `duration`, `delay`, `radius`, `path`, `reverse`: Animation properties passed to `OrbitingCircles`. - `component`: A function that returns the React node for the icon. ### `AnimatedForm` A versatile form component with animation and validation capabilities. It supports headers, sub-headers, input fields, Google sign-in, and custom navigation. **Props:** - `header`: The main header text for the form. - `subHeader`: Optional sub-header text. - `fields`: An array of `Field` objects, defining the input fields. - `submitButton`: Text for the submit button. - `textVariantButton`: Text for an alternative action button (e.g., 'Forgot Password?'). - `errorField`: Displays a general error message above the submit button. - `fieldPerRow`: The number of fields to display per row on larger screens. - `onSubmit`: Callback function triggered on successful form submission. - `googleLogin`: Text for the Google login button. - `onGoogleLogin`: Callback function for Google login. - `goTo`: Callback function for the alternative action button. **`Field` Interface:** - `label`: The label text for the input. - `required`: Boolean indicating if the field is required. - `type`: The input type ('text', 'email', 'password'). - `placeholder`: Placeholder text for the input. - `onChange`: Callback function for input change events. ### `BottomGradient` A decorative component that adds subtle gradient effects, typically used for buttons to enhance their visual appeal on hover. ## Usage Example ```tsx import React, { useState } from 'react'; import { AnimatedForm, Input, Label, BoxReveal } from './components/ui/modern-animated-sign-in'; const SignInPage: React.FC = () => { const [email, setEmail] = useState(''); const [password, setPassword] = useState(''); const handleSignIn = (event: React.FormEvent) => { event.preventDefault(); console.log('Signing in with:', email, password); // Add your sign-in logic here }; return (
setEmail(e.target.value) }, { label: 'Password', type: 'password', placeholder: '••••••••', required: true, onChange: (e) => setPassword(e.target.value) }, ]} submitButton="Sign In" googleLogin="Sign in with Google" onGoogleLogin={() => console.log('Google login clicked')} textVariantButton="Forgot Password?" goTo={() => console.log('Go to forgot password')} onSubmit={handleSignIn} />
); }; export default SignInPage; ```
